surface.name: Groq API Quickstart via cURL (Responses)
surface.selection_basis: Groq's quickstart documents a cURL POST to https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/responses with Authorization: Bearer $GROQ_API_KEY alongside Python and JavaScript OpenAI-compatible clients. An SDK install is not required for the first authenticated Responses call. Billing FAQs describe a Free tier that can call the API; payment methods are required only when upgrading to the Developer tier.

alternatives_considered.name: Python OpenAI-compatible client
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Optional convenience client against the same OpenAI-compatible base URL.

alternatives_considered.name: JavaScript OpenAI-compatible client
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Peer SDK tab; same endpoint as the documented cURL example.

alternatives_considered.name: Developer tier upgrade
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Higher limits and pay-as-you-go billing after Free-tier first success.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a GroqCloud API key and receive a Responses API JSON result over HTTPS without installing an SDK.
assumed_prior_state: No GroqCloud account or API key is assumed.
A terminal with cURL is available; no language SDK is assumed.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated cURL POST to https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/responses returns model output text.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Responses API returns a successful JSON body with model-generated text for the quickstart prompt.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The quickstart documents Create an API key, export GROQ_API_KEY, and a cURL POST to https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/responses.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The first meaningful LLM success is a successful authenticated Responses API response. cURL is documented on the same quickstart page as the SDK tabs, so package installation is optional. Free-tier accounts can call the API; a payment method is documented for Developer-tier upgrade, not for the Free-tier first call.

prerequisites.requirement: A GroqCloud account that can open API Keys in the console.
An API key created in the Groq console and stored securely.
A terminal with cURL (or any HTTP client) able to send HTTPS JSON requests. No language SDK is required.

primary_path.output: Documented first-call path with cURL and SDK tabs
primary_path.success_signal: The cURL Responses example is visible.

primary_path.action: Sign up for or sign in to GroqCloud.
details: Treat signup as one opaque step at the official console login/signup flow.
The public Login shell says continuing accepts the Groq Services Agreement.
primary_path.input: Intent to use the Groq API
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Follow the displayed account recovery path.

primary_path.action: Create and copy an API key.
details: Open API Keys in the console and create a key.
primary_path.success_signal: The key value is available locally.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No manual approval wait is documented for key creation.

primary_path.action: Export the key as GROQ_API_KEY in the terminal.
primary_path.output: API key available to HTTP clients in the shell
primary_path.success_signal: The environment variable is set for the current shell.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Shell persistence depends on the OS.

primary_path.action: POST a Responses API request with cURL to https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/responses.
details: Include Content-Type: application/json and Authorization: Bearer $GROQ_API_KEY.
Send a JSON body with a model and input prompt as shown in the quickstart cURL example.
primary_path.success_signal: The API returns a successful JSON body containing model output text.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing key, rate limits, or invalid JSON produce API errors instead of model output.

primary_path.action: Confirm the Responses API JSON includes the model-generated text.
primary_path.success_signal: Model output text is present in the successful response.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An error body means the first call did not succeed.

branches.condition: The developer prefers an official SDK instead of cURL.
branches.path: Use the Python or JavaScript OpenAI-compatible client against https://api.groq.com/openai/v1 and call responses.create with the same model and input; the API key requirement stays the same.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds optional local package installation without changing the authenticated Responses boundary.

branches.condition: The developer needs higher Free-tier rate limits.
branches.path: Upgrade to the Developer tier in billing settings and provide a valid payment method; billing then follows progressive thresholds and monthly invoices.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Optional after first success; not required for a Free-tier Responses call.

friction_gates.description: API key creation requires an authenticated GroqCloud console session.
Continuing through Login accepts the Groq Services Agreement.
A secret API key must be created and stored safely.
Free-tier accounts are subject to published Free plan rate limits.

sources.title: Groq API Quickstart
evidence_supported: cURL POST to /openai/v1/responses with Bearer token

sources.title: Billing FAQs
evidence_supported: Free tier exists before Developer upgrade
Payment method required to upgrade to Developer tier

sources.title: Rate Limits
evidence_supported: Free plan rate limits for API usage

uncertainties.question: Whether every brand-new Free-tier account can complete a Responses call without any promotional-credit or region-specific gate beyond rate limits.
uncertainties.impact: Could add a rare billing or access gate before first success for some accounts.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Billing FAQs describe Free vs Developer clearly but do not enumerate every fresh-account edge case.

excluded_after_success: Developer tier upgrade and payment method
